headline: MP Materials posts Q4 profit, unveils $1.25B Texas magnet factory plans
classifier_reasoning: Exact match to rare_earth_national_security archetype with DoD strategic contract and major domestic manufacturing capex, no existing active theme for this specific US rare earth onshoring wave.
raw_content: MP Materials Corp (NYSE: MP) reported a profitable fourth quarter driven largely by a $51 million non-revenue Price Protection Agreement from the US Department of War, as the rare-earths miner also announced plans for a major new manufacturing campus in Texas. The company posted net income of $9.4 million in Q4 2025, alongside adjusted EBITDA of $39.2 million, marking its first profitable quarter of the year.

headline: MP Materials Revenue Falls After Halting Rare Earth Sales to China
classifier_reasoning: [batch-dedup] Exact archetype match (rare_earth_national_security) with clear U.S. government policy involvement and material supply chain impact, no existing theme covers this specific MP Materials export halt event
raw_content: The company's revenue declined 14% following the decision, which was made as part of an agreement with the U.S. government.

headline: MP Materials Selects Northlake, Texas, as the Site of “10X,” a New U.S. Rare Earth Magnet Manufacturing Campus
classifier_reasoning: [batch-dedup] Exact match to rare_earth_national_security archetype with MP's major domestic magnet manufacturing expansion announcement, no existing active theme for this specific capacity buildout event.
raw_content: NORTHLAKE, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $MP #rareearth--MP Materials Corp. (NYSE: MP) today announced it has selected a 120‑acre site in Northlake, Texas, to develop “10X,” the company's planned large-scale rare earth magnet manufacturing campus. Located less than 10 miles from MP's existing Independence facility in Fort Worth, the new campus will cement North Texas as the center of gravity for the United States' rare earth magnet supply chain. 10X will significantly expand MP's fully integrated U.S. rare earth mag.

headline: MP Materials Backed By $110 DoD Price Floor As JPMorgan Sees 33% Upside
classifier_reasoning: [batch-dedup] DoD price floor represents concrete government policy action (not just discussion) supporting US rare earth supply chain, matching rare_earth_national_security archetype with specific MP Materials catalyst and no overlapping active theme.
raw_content: Rare earths are political again. And that's exactly why MP Materials Corp (NYSE: MP) may have one of the most unusual earnings setups in mining: a government-backed floor with upside still intact.

headline: MP Materials: Advancing On National Security And Government-Backed Cash Flow Floor
classifier_reasoning: [batch-dedup] Exact match to rare_earth_national_security archetype with specific DoD price protection policy, no existing theme covers this government-backed revenue floor mechanism
raw_content: MP Materials earns a strong buy rating, driven by the Department of War Price Protection Agreement guaranteeing a $110/kg NdPr revenue floor from October 2025. The PPA decouples MP's cash flow from volatile spot prices and midstream bottlenecks, transforming inventory into a high-margin, government-backed asset. Key risks include downstream yield losses at the Independence facility, which could erode margin gains if recycling and manufacturing efficiencies lag.

headline: Rare earth stocks jump after Trump launches $12 billion critical minerals stockpile
classifier_reasoning: [batch-dedup] Exact archetype match with clear government policy action establishing strategic reserve, no existing active theme for this specific Trump stockpile initiative
raw_content: The moves come shortly after President Donald Trump announced the establishment of a U.S. critical mineral reserve.

headline: Trump Admin To Acquire Stake In USA Rare Earth For $1.6 Billion: Report
classifier_reasoning: Trump admin $1.6B investment in USA Rare Earth is a direct continuation of the US rare earth onshoring theme triggered by MP Materials DoD deal 4 days ago, same archetype, same strategic policy driver, adds new government commitment scale.
raw_content: The Trump administration plans to invest $1.6 billion in USA Rare Earth Inc. to secure essential mineral supplies.
